assessmentObjectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge of locations, places, processes, environments and different scales
Demonstrate geographical understanding of concepts and how they relate to different contexts
Apply knowledge and understanding to interpret, analyse and evaluate geographical information and issues
Select, adapt and use a variety of skills and techniques to investigate questions and issues

topics.name: Natural Hazards
topics.description: Definition of natural hazards, types (geological, meteorological), and factors affecting hazard risk

topics.name: Plate Tectonics
topics.description: Plate tectonics theory, global distribution of earthquakes and volcanoes, plate margins

topics.name: Tectonic Hazards: Effects and Responses
topics.description: Primary and secondary effects, immediate and long-term responses, contrasting wealth

topics.name: Managing Tectonic Hazards
topics.description: Reasons people live in at-risk areas, monitoring, prediction, protection and planning

topics.name: Global Atmospheric Circulation
topics.description: General atmospheric circulation model, pressure belts, surface winds and climate patterns

topics.name: Tropical Storms
topics.description: Global distribution, formation and development of tropical storms, structure and features

topics.name: Tropical Storms: Effects and Responses
topics.description: Effects of tropical storms, responses using a named example, monitoring and prediction

topics.name: UK Weather Hazards
topics.description: Types of UK weather hazard, extreme weather events and their impacts

topics.name: Climate Change
topics.description: Evidence for climate change, natural and human causes

topics.name: Managing Climate Change
topics.description: Mitigation and adaptation strategies for climate change

topics.name: Ecosystems
topics.description: Ecosystems at different scales, biotic and abiotic components, food chains and nutrient cycling

topics.name: Global Ecosystems and Biomes
topics.description: Distribution and characteristics of large-scale global ecosystems

topics.name: Tropical Rainforests
topics.description: Physical characteristics, interdependence, plant and animal adaptations, biodiversity

topics.name: Deforestation of Tropical Rainforests
topics.description: Rates and causes of deforestation, economic and environmental impacts

topics.name: Sustainable Rainforest Management
topics.description: Value of rainforests, strategies for sustainable management

topics.name: Hot Deserts
topics.description: Physical characteristics, adaptations, development opportunities and challenges

topics.name: Desertification
topics.description: Causes and strategies to reduce the risk of desertification

topics.name: Cold Environments
topics.description: Physical characteristics of polar and tundra environments, adaptations and opportunities

topics.name: Managing Cold Environments
topics.description: Value of cold environments, balancing development and conservation

topics.name: UK Physical Landscapes
topics.description: Overview of UK landscapes, upland and lowland areas, river systems

topics.name: Coastal Processes
topics.description: Wave types, weathering, mass movement, erosion, transportation and deposition

topics.name: Coastal Landforms
topics.description: How geology influences coastal forms, erosion and deposition landforms

topics.name: Coastal Management
topics.description: Hard and soft engineering, managed retreat, UK case study

topics.name: River Processes
topics.description: Long profile, fluvial erosion, transportation and deposition

topics.name: River Landforms
topics.description: Erosion and deposition landforms including meanders and flood plains

topics.name: Flood Risk and Management
topics.description: Physical and human factors, hydrographs, hard and soft engineering, UK case study

topics.name: Glacial Processes and Landforms
topics.description: Glacial erosion, transportation, deposition and resulting landforms

topics.name: Glacial Tourism and Management
topics.description: Economic activities in glaciated uplands, conflicts and management

topics.name: Urbanisation and Megacities
topics.description: Global pattern of urban change, factors affecting urbanisation, emergence of megacities

topics.name: Urban Growth in LICs and NEEs
topics.description: Case study of a major city in an LIC/NEE: opportunities, challenges and planning

topics.name: Urban Change in the UK
topics.description: UK city case study: migration impacts, opportunities, challenges and regeneration

topics.name: Sustainable Urban Living
topics.description: Features of sustainable urban living and transport strategies

topics.name: Measuring Development
topics.description: Economic and social measures of development, limitations, Demographic Transition Model

topics.name: Uneven Development and its Consequences
topics.description: Causes and consequences of uneven development

topics.name: Reducing the Development Gap
topics.description: Strategies to reduce the development gap including aid, trade and investment

topics.name: Economic Development in LICs and NEEs
topics.description: Case study: changing industrial structure, TNCs, aid, environmental impacts

topics.name: Economic Change in the UK
topics.description: De-industrialisation, post-industrial economy, north-south divide, UK in the wider world

topics.name: Resource Management Overview
topics.description: Significance of food, water and energy, global inequalities in supply and consumption

topics.name: Food Resources
topics.description: Global patterns, factors affecting supply, strategies to increase supply, sustainability

topics.name: Water Resources
topics.description: Surplus and deficit patterns, availability factors, strategies, sustainability

topics.name: Energy Resources
topics.description: Global distribution of consumption and supply, renewable and non-renewable, sustainability

topics.name: Issue Evaluation
topics.description: Critical thinking using pre-release resources, stakeholder analysis, reasoned justifications

topics.name: Fieldwork: Enquiry Process
topics.description: Formulating questions, selecting evidence, identifying locations, risk assessment

topics.name: Fieldwork: Data Collection and Presentation
topics.description: Primary and secondary data, sampling methods, presentation techniques

topics.name: Fieldwork: Analysis, Conclusions and Evaluation
topics.description: Data analysis, statistical techniques, drawing conclusions, evaluating enquiries

topics.name: Cartographic Skills
topics.description: Atlas maps, OS maps, grid references, scale, distance, gradient, contours, sketch maps

topics.name: Graphical and Numerical Skills
topics.description: Constructing and interpreting graphs, numerical skills, proportion and ratio

topics.name: Statistical Skills and Data Analysis
topics.description: Measures of central tendency, bivariate data, trend lines, identifying weaknesses
